regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean weather for March 27, 2026: San Juan reports 74°F with light southeast winds at 4 knots and overcast skies, with minimal rain chance today. Expect increasing easterly winds 10-13 knots and a rising rain threat through San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, Antigua, and Turks & Caicos over the next three days, including thunderstorms tomorrow with up to 78% rain probability. Mariners should prepare for reduced visibility and choppy seas, especially near exposed anchorages in Antigua and Turks & Caicos. Fishing captains and yacht operators should secure gear and monitor weather updates closely as scattered thunderstorms may produce sudden gusts and brief heavy showers.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean outlook for March 27, 2026: Current conditions in Cozumel show 77°F with ENE winds at 10 knots, partly cloudy skies, and no rain expected. Winds will increase to 14-16 knots from the ENE to NE over the next two days, with a slight chance of light showers (up to 15%) mainly offshore. By day three, expect overcast skies and lighter rain chances near 5 percent. Mariners should note moderate easterly trade winds favoring smooth seas; no Small Craft Advisories or warnings are in effect. Fishing captains in shallow anchorages such as Belize City and Roatan should secure gear against steady trade winds, while cruise passengers visiting Cozumel and Costa Maya can plan outdoor activities with minimal weather disruptions.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region weather for March 27, 2026: Currently, Nassau reports clear skies, 68°F, with light ENE winds at 5 knots and zero rain chance. Expect a mild day with ENE winds increasing to 10 knots and partly cloudy skies through Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, Berry Islands, and Turks & Caicos. By day three, winds strengthen to 22 knots ENE with a 25% chance of rain; small craft should prepare for choppier seas, especially near exposed anchorages in Turks & Caicos. Fishing captains should plan morning trips for calmer conditions, and cruise passengers can enjoy outdoor activities today and tomorrow but monitor updates for increasing winds and scattered showers later in the week.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean briefing for March 27, 2026: Aruba reports steady east winds at 16 knots, temperature near 79°F, with partly cloudy skies and minimal rain chance today. Expect overcast conditions and east winds increasing to 19 knots tomorrow with a 20% chance of light showers; winds ease slightly to 15-16 knots by day three with rain chances below 10%. Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena should anticipate similar easterly trade winds and mostly stable weather with low rain risk. Small craft operators should exercise caution in open waters east of Aruba as winds approach advisory thresholds; secure loose gear and monitor for localized squalls near shorelines. Passengers planning beach or fishing excursions will find today favorable, but overcast skies and brief sprinkles may limit visibility and comfort later in the week at all ports.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean conditions today feature steady northeast to east-northeast winds at 15-21 knots with overcast skies and low to moderate rain chances increasing through the period. Grand Cayman is currently 79°F with 17-knot NE winds and a 15% rain chance, trending to 80°F with scattered showers and a 38% likelihood tonight, rising to 68% on March 28. Expect seas to build but remain below Small Craft Advisory thresholds; however, gusty showers may cause brief visibility reductions. Captains should secure light gear and monitor radar for localized downpours, especially near Belize City and Cozumel where showers are most frequent. Passengers are advised to carry rain gear ashore and expect intermittent wet conditions at all ports through the next 72 hours.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles weather for March 27, 2026: Conditions remain stable with steady east to east-northeast trade winds at 12-16 knots, overcast skies, and minimal rain chances under 10 percent from Barbados to Grenada. Expect seas to be moderate with no Small Craft Advisories in effect. Yacht and fishing captains should maintain routine eastward headings, particularly near exposed anchorages in St. Lucia and Martinique, where easterly swell may increase vessel motion. Passengers should prepare for warm, humid conditions with light overcast skies and brief afternoon showers possible by day three, especially near St. Vincent and Grenada.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles and adjacent ports will see mild conditions today with light WSW winds near 4 knots under persistent fog and zero precipitation. Expect afternoon highs near 71 degrees with winds increasing to 10 knots from the WSW; fog may reduce visibility in harbor areas including Los Angeles/Long Beach and Marina del Rey, so exercise caution when maneuvering. Over the next two days, winds shift to WNW and back to WSW near 9-11 knots with overcast skies but no rain; Catalina Island waters remain calm for safe anchorage and day trips. Mariners should maintain vigilant navigation in foggy conditions today and prepare for improved visibility and moderate winds through the weekend.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region current conditions are mild with light SSW winds at 2 knots and partly cloudy skies; no precipitation expected today. Visibility may be reduced by fog near Ensenada and Punta Banda tomorrow and the day after, so mariners should exercise caution when approaching these ports during early morning hours. Winds remain light to moderate through the 3-day period, peaking near 8 knots from the southwest, with no marine wind warnings in effect. Fishing and yacht captains should plan for calm seas but monitor for fog-related visibility restrictions, especially near Islas Todos Santos where overcast conditions develop by day three.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas region conditions remain stable today with temperatures near 72°F and northwest winds steady at 11 knots. Skies are partly cloudy with no precipitation expected through the next three days, though overcast conditions will dominate. Winds will hold between 11 and 13 knots from the northwest to west-northwest, well below Small Craft Advisory thresholds. Mariners should expect smooth seas; yacht captains can maintain normal anchorage operations in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz. Cruise passengers should enjoy outdoor activities but prepare for reduced sunshine under persistent cloud cover.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan region currently reports light SSE winds at 5 knots with partly cloudy skies and no rain. Expect increasing SW winds up to 10 knots today with a 23% chance of isolated rain showers, shifting to overcast WNW winds near 10 knots tomorrow with similar rain chances. By day three, winds remain WNW around 8 knots with overcast skies and minimal rain risk. Mariners should anticipate generally safe conditions for navigation and anchoring in Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata, but fishing captains should prepare for possible brief showers today and tomorrow; cruise passengers should carry light rain gear for occasional showers. No marine wind warnings are in effect.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta region is currently stable with light NNW winds at 2 knots, overcast skies, and no rain expected today. Winds will shift WNW at 8-10 knots over the next two days, remaining overcast with minimal rain risk through Day 2. By Day 3, thunderstorms are likely with a 63% chance of rain and sustained W winds near 8 knots. Captains should maintain secure anchorage in all ports, especially Yelapa and Chacala, where thunderstorms could produce sudden gusts; passengers are advised to plan outdoor activities early in the day to avoid late afternoon storms.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean briefing for March 27, 2026: Barcelona reports 50°F with light northwest winds at 4 knots under partly cloudy skies and minimal rain risk. Through Day 2, expect gradual warming to the low 60s with light to moderate southwest to west-southwest winds and mostly overcast conditions; rain chances remain low under 10 percent. By Day 3, winds shift north-northeast near 12 knots with an increased 28 percent chance of thunderstorms—yacht and fishing captains should monitor updates closely for potential sudden squalls near Palma de Mallorca and Ibiza. Cruise passengers should plan outdoor activities for the next two days but remain cautious of developing storms later in the week, especially when approaching Malaga, Cadiz, Gibraltar, and Lisbon where sea conditions may become unsettled.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean weather for March 27, 2026: Naples and surrounding ports will see mild temperatures near 51°F this morning with light NNE winds at 1 knot and partly cloudy skies. Expect increasing cloud cover and a 50% chance of light rain by afternoon with northerly winds shifting to 5-8 knots over the next three days. Mariners should prepare for overcast conditions and reduced visibility, especially near Naples, Civitavecchia, and Livorno, where wet decks and slick surfaces may impact small craft operations. Yacht and fishing captains should monitor forecasts closely for possible rain on Day 1 and avoid exposed anchorages around Sardinia and Corsica during the light northerly breeze; no advisories or warnings are in effect. Cruise passengers should carry rain gear when ashore and anticipate cooler evenings through the period.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Eastern Mediterranean, March 27, 2026: Athens (Piraeus) reports mild conditions at 62°F with WSW winds 11 knots and partly cloudy skies. A 5% chance of rain today makes for generally favorable navigation and passenger activities; however, expect increasing shower chances (58%) tomorrow with SW winds rising to 13 knots, which may impact small craft operations near Athens and Santorini. By day three, lighter winds from the NNW at 5 knots and fog development warrant caution for vessel traffic around Mykonos and Rhodes; reduced visibility could affect docking and anchorage safety. Fishing captains should plan for calm seas today but monitor forecasts closely for tomorrow’s rain and overcast conditions particularly near Izmir and Cyprus.
